00604| Macro Expansion
00605| ---------------
00606| 
00607| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00608| 
00609| :dp:`fls_xscdaxvs4wx4`
00610| :dt:`Macro expansion` is the process of statically executing a
00611| :t:`macro invocation` and replacing it with the produced output of the
00612| :t:`macro invocation`.
00613| 
00614| :dp:`fls_nz5stwcc41gk`
00615| :t:`Macro expansion` of :t:`[declarative macro]s` proceeds as follows:
00616| 
00617| #. :dp:`fls_40xq8Ri1OMZZ`
00618|    The :s:`TokenTree` of the :t:`macro invocation` has all
00619|    :t:`[outer block doc]s` and :t:`[outer line doc]s` contained within replaced
00620|    by their equivalent :t:`attribute` :c:`doc` representation.
00621| 
00622| #. :dp:`fls_76prdp6k1fga`
00623|    The :s:`TokenTree` of the :t:`macro invocation` is matched against the
00624|    :t:`[macro rule]s` of the resolved :t:`macro` by considering individual
00625|    :t:`[macro matcher]s`. It is a static error if no :t:`macro matcher` is
00626|    satisfied.
00627| 
00628| #. :dp:`fls_76u274l4kew8`
00629|    The :t:`macro transcriber` of the satisfied :t:`macro rule` produces its
00630|    result, with all :t:`[metavariable indication]s` resolved. It is a static
00631|    error if the :t:`macro transcriber` fails to produce its result.
00632| 
00633| #. :dp:`fls_lakpily1zwfl`
00634|    The :t:`macro invocation` is replaced with the result of the
00635|    :t:`macro transcriber`. It is a static error if the result cannot be parsed
00636|    according to the expected expansion syntax of the context where the
00637|    :t:`macro invocation` resides. The expected expansion syntax is as follows:
00638| 
00639|    #. :dp:`fls_y20pmwo3v3uu`
00640|       If the :t:`macro invocation` appears as part of an :t:`associated item`,
00641|       an :t:`item` within an :t:`external block`, or another
00642|       :t:`macro invocation`, the output is required to constitute zero or more
00643|       :t:`[item]s`.
00644| 
00645|    #. :dp:`fls_nsh2vwx8oiw`
00646|       If the :t:`macro invocation` appears as part of an
00647|       :dt:`expression-without-block`, the output is required to constitute an
00648|       :t:`expression`.
00649| 
00650|    #. :dp:`fls_tu6kmwm4v9nj`
00651|       If the :t:`macro invocation` appears as part of a
00652|       :dt:`pattern-without-range`, the output is required to constitute a
00653|       :t:`[pattern]`.
00654| 
00655|    #. :dp:`fls_3zn4dz19nyvq`
00656|       If the :t:`macro invocation` appears as part of a :t:`statement`, the
00657|       output is required to constitute zero or more :t:`[statement]s`.
00658|

00660|       If the :t:`macro invocation` appears as part of a
00661|       :t:`type specification` without :t:`[bound]s`, the output is required to
00662|       constitute a :t:`type`.
00663| 
00664| :dp:`fls_417hvhvj2554`

00666|

00112| A :dt:`subject expression` is an :t:`expression` that controls :t:`[for loop]s`,

00140| :dp:`fls_oqj7s9fi3j3j`
00141| An :dt:`assignee expression` is an :t:`expression` that appears as the

00254| 
00255| Where Clauses
00256| -------------
00257| 
00258| .. rubric:: Syntax
00259| 
00260| .. syntax::
00261| 
00262|    WhereClause ::=
00263|        $$where$$ WhereClausePredicateList
00264| 
00265|    WhereClausePredicateList ::=
00266|        WhereClausePredicate ($$,$$ WhereClausePredicate)* $$,$$?
00267| 
00268|    WhereClausePredicate ::=
00269|        LifetimeBoundPredicate
00270|      | TypeBoundPredicate
00271| 
00272|    LifetimeBoundPredicate ::=
00273|       LifetimeIndication $$:$$ LifetimeIndicationList?
00274| 
00275|    TypeBoundPredicate ::=
00276|       ForGenericParameterList? TypeSpecification $$:$$ TypeBoundList?
00277| 
00278| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00279| 
00280| :dp:`fls_fhy4rsmmbvyy`
00281| A :dt:`where clause predicate` is either a :t:`lifetime bound predicate` or a
00282| :t:`type bound predicate`.
00283| 
00284| :dp:`fls_V4PKFqtCsAv6`
00285| A :dt:`lifetime bound predicate` is a :t:`construct` that specifies
00286| :t:`[lifetime bound]s` on a :t:`lifetime parameter`.
00287| 
00288| :dp:`fls_cslGPmVjujHD`
00289| A :t:`type bound predicate` is a :t:`construct` that specifies
00290| :t:`[lifetime bound]s` and :t:`[trait bound]s` on a :t:`type`.
00291| 
00292| :dp:`fls_ytk74dyxuy6d`
00293| A :t:`construct` is valid when all of its :t:`[where clause predicate]s` hold
00294| true for the supplied :t:`[generic argument]s`.
00295| 
00296| :dp:`fls_1xgw1dq60quz`
00297| A :dt:`trivial predicate` is a :t:`where clause predicate` that does not use
00298| the :t:`[generic parameter]s` or :t:`[higher-ranked trait bound]s` of the related
00299| :t:`construct`.
00300| 
00301| :dp:`fls_47s8i7pzb9gg`
00302| It is a static error to create a :t:`trivial predicate` that does not hold.

00315| -----------------

00318| 
00319| .. syntax::
00320| 
00321|    GenericArgumentList ::=

00323| 
00324|    GenericArgument ::=

00326|      | BindingBoundArgument

00329|      | TypeArgument

00335|        Identifier $$:$$ TypeBoundList

00338|        BlockExpression

00344|

00348| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00349| 
00350| :dp:`fls_3x6qd8vt5uus`
00351| A :dt:`generic argument` supplies a static input for an
00352| :t:`associated trait type` or a :t:`generic parameter`.

00354| :dp:`fls_8j3ARLPm65vI`
00355| A :t:`generic substitution` is the replacement of a :t:`generic parameter`
00356| with a :t:`generic argument`.
00357| 
00358| :dp:`fls_ky39fb2vcom6`
00359| A :s:`BindingArgument` shall follow :s:`[ConstantArgument]s`,
00360| :s:`[LifetimeArgument]s`, and :s:`[TypeArgument]s` in a
00361| :s:`GenericArgumentList`.
00362| 
00363| :dp:`fls_9n1ejjili06h`
00364| A :s:`LifetimeArgument` shall precede :s:`[BindingArgument]s`,
00365| :s:`[ConstantArgument]s`, and :s:`[TypeArgument]s` in a

00369| A :dt:`binding argument` is a :t:`generic argument` that supplies the :t:`type`

00371|

00373| A :dt:`binding bound argument` is a :t:`generic argument` that further imposes

00387| A :t:`constant argument` may only appear as a single segment

00432| :dt:`Generic conformance` measures the compatibility between a set of

00132| A :t:`mutable reference` is a :t:`value` of a :t:`mutable reference type`, and
00133| allows the mutation of its :t:`referent`.
00134| 
00135| :dp:`fls_hqxsuyn285he`
00136| The :t:`referent` of an :t:`immutable reference` shall be mutated only when the
00137| :t:`type` of the :t:`referent` is subject to :t:`interior mutability`.
00138| 
00139| :dp:`fls_i1ny0k726a4a`
00140| While a :t:`mutable reference` is :t:`active`, no other :t:`reference` shall
00141| refer to a :t:`value` that :t:`[overlap]s` with the :t:`referent` of the
00142| :t:`mutable reference`.
00143| 
00144| .. rubric:: Undefined Behavior
00145| 
00146| :dp:`fls_eT1hnLOx6vxk`
00147| It is undefined behavior to access a :t:`value` through aliasing
00148| :t:`[mutable reference]s` from :t:`unsafe context`.
00149| 
00150| .. rubric:: Examples
00151| 
00152| .. code-block:: rust
00153| 
00154|    let immutable_reference: &i32 = &42;
00155|    let mutable_reference: &mut i32 = &mut 42;
00156| 
00157| .. _fls_a14slch83hzn:
00158| 
00159| Borrowing
00160| ---------
00161| 
00162| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00163| 
00164| :dp:`fls_c02flohk54pc`
00165| :dt:`Borrowing` is the process of temporarily associating a :t:`reference` with a
00166| :t:`value` without transferring :t:`ownership` permanently.
00167| 
00168| :dp:`fls_j9kof0px3l7s`
00169| A :dt:`borrow` is a :t:`reference` produced by :t:`borrowing`.
00170| 
00171| :dp:`fls_yL6cMahJUIqO`
00172| A :t:`value` is :t:`borrowed` when it is associated with an active
00173| :t:`borrow`.
00174| 
00175| :dp:`fls_zepwytjwy049`
00176| An :dt:`implicit borrow` is a :t:`borrow` that is not present syntactically in
00177| program text. An :t:`implicit borrow` occurs in the following contexts:
00178| 
00179| * :dp:`fls_nordokzfy36d`
00180|   The :t:`call operand` of a :t:`call expression`,
00181| 
00182| * :dp:`fls_bjf3futso849`
00183|   The :t:`[operand]s` of a :t:`comparison expression`,
00184| 
00185| * :dp:`fls_yfmy4v5zlgw9`
00186|   The :t:`assigned operand` of a :t:`compound assignment expression`,
00187| 
00188| * :dp:`fls_jv18y618j2s3`
00189|   The :t:`operand` of a :t:`field access expression`,
00190| 
00191| * :dp:`fls_g4i0jb27iryr`
00192|   The :t:`operand` of a :t:`dereference expression`,
00193| 
00194| * :dp:`fls_kky9ufexrvaw`
00195|   The :t:`indexed operand` of an :t:`index expression`,
00196| 
00197| * :dp:`fls_o5oq4jfswr4q`
00198|   The :t:`receiver operand` of a :t:`method call expression`.
00199| 
00200| :dp:`fls_MT69AGRXH9pS``
00201| An :t:`implicit borrow` may be an :t:`immutable borrow` or a :t:`mutable borrow`
00202| if required.
00203| 
00204| :dp:`fls_hyl4bdjbuzbw`
00205| An :dt:`immutable borrow` is an :t:`immutable reference` produced by
00206| :t:`borrowing`.
00207| 
00208| :dp:`fls_8q0n7v3k2m1b`
00209| A :dt:`shared borrow` is a :t:`borrow` produced by evaluating an
00210| :t:`immutable borrow expression`.
00211| 
00212| :dp:`fls_pu19i4sj6yg0`
00213| A :dt:`mutable borrow` is a :t:`mutable reference` produced by :t:`borrowing`.
00214| 
00215| :dp:`fls_kxws4zmaahj6`
00216| :t:`Borrowing` a :t:`field` of a :t:`union type` borrows all remaining
00217| :t:`[field]s` using the same :t:`lifetime`.
00218| 
00219| :dp:`fls_kup2ou22nwyl`
00220| Immutably :t:`borrowing` a :t:`value` proceeds as follows:
00221| 
00222| #. :dp:`fls_8q5ly4x104ai`
00223|    An :t:`immutable borrow` of :t:`type` ``&'a T`` is created, where
00224|    :t:`lifetime` ``'a`` is replaced by a :t:`lifetime variable`, and
00225|    ``T`` is replaced by the borrowed :t:`type`.
00226| 
00227| #. :dp:`fls_yhchu2bpil4m`
00228|    Lifetime inference is performed.
00229| 
00230| #. :dp:`fls_568o7nyihndd`
00231|    The :t:`immutable borrow` is checked against other :t:`[borrow]s` and
00232|    :t:`by move` passing within the enclosing :t:`item`.
00233| 
00234| #. :dp:`fls_Z2n7EJiLY5CT`
00235|    An :t:`immutable reference` to the borrowed :t:`value` is produced.
00236| 
00237| #. :dp:`fls_ibi9mqGeHNLp`
00238|    The :t:`immutable borrow` is released immediately after the last usage of its
00239|    related :t:`immutable reference`.
00240| 
00241| :dp:`fls_f9we73i8vwq3`
00242| Mutably :t:`borrowing` a :t:`value` proceeds as follows:
00243| 
00244| #. :dp:`fls_w5bjgaov8w60`
00245|    A :t:`mutable borrow` of :t:`type` ``&'a mut T`` is created, where
00246|    :t:`lifetime` ``'a`` is replaced by a :t:`lifetime variable`, and
00247|    ``T`` is replaced by the borrowed :t:`type`.
00248| 
00249| #. :dp:`fls_gbqizu6gu6kk`
00250|    Lifetime inference is performed.
00251| 
00252| #. :dp:`fls_ovkkxeybumvt`
00253|    The :t:`mutable borrow` is checked against other :t:`[borrow]s` and
00254|    :t:`by move` passing within the enclosing :t:`item`.
00255| 
00256| #. :dp:`fls_7Kzu0qXiBUCb`
00257|    A :t:`mutable reference` to the borrowed :t:`value` is produced.
00258| 
00259| #. :dp:`fls_VDHBP4oAoqT5`
00260|    The :t:`mutable borrow` is released immediately after the last usage of its
00261|    related :t:`mutable reference`.
00262| 
00263| .. rubric:: Examples
00264| 
00265| .. code-block:: rust
00266| 
00267|    let mutable_borrow = &mut 42;
00268|    let immutable_borrow = &42;
00269| 
00270| .. _fls_77scxuomlbgs:
00271| 
00272| Passing Conventions
00273| -------------------
00274| 
00275| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00276| 
00277| :dp:`fls_fvwx2ufeyzcs`
00278| A :dt:`passing convention` is the mechanism that defines how a :t:`value` is
00279| transferred between :t:`[place]s`.
00280| 
00281| :dp:`fls_h2pgsij1rbms`
00282| A :dt:`copy type` is a :t:`type` that implements the :std:`core::marker::Copy`
00283| :t:`trait`.
00284| 
00285| :dp:`fls_yx2knbby70fy`
00286| A :t:`value` of a :t:`copy type` is :t:`passed <passing convention>`
00287| :dt:`by copy`. Passing :t:`by copy` does not change the :t:`owner` of the
00288| :t:`value`.
00289| 
00290| :dp:`fls_6ul3f6v0foma`
00291| A :dt:`move type` is a :t:`type` that implements the :std:`core::marker::Sized`
00292| :t:`trait` and is not a :t:`copy type`.
00293| 
00294| :dp:`fls_3ztdz02efeoc`
00295| A :t:`value` of a :t:`move type` is :t:`passed <passing convention>`
00296| :dt:`by move`. Passing :t:`by move` changes the :t:`owner` of the :t:`value`.
00297| 
00298| :dp:`fls_konzgoybhfqm`
00299| A :t:`value` of a :t:`place expression` shall be
00300| :t:`passed <passing convention>` :t:`by move` only when it denotes:
00301| 
00302| * :dp:`fls_4bnbv7mqod57`
00303|   A :t:`field` of a :t:`place expression` that can be
00304|   :t:`passed <passing convention>` :t:`by move` and whose :t:`type` does not
00305|   implement the :std:`core::ops::Drop` :t:`trait`, or
00306| 
00307| * :dp:`fls_3xk3p1unbjy5`
00308|   A :t:`temporary`, or
00309| 
00310| * :dp:`fls_vk1xhvdaakh0`
00311|   A :t:`variable` which is not currently :t:`borrowed`.
00312| 
00313| :dp:`fls_vveEJn7lngT8`
00314| A :t:`value` of a :t:`value expression` is always
00315| :t:`passed <passing convention>` :t:`by move`.
00316| 
00317| :dp:`fls_gq35gqagw35`
00318| A :t:`value` not subject to :t:`by copy` or :t:`by move` :t:`passing convention`
00319| shall not be :t:`passed <passing convention>` between :t:`[place]s`.
00320| 
00321| .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
00322| 
00323| :dp:`fls_cfqzpmnfeh8h`
00324| Passing a :t:`value` :t:`by copy` from a source :t:`owner` to a target
00325| :t:`owner` proceeds as follows:
00326| 
00327| #. :dp:`fls_go9gdlk5d3km`
00328|    The :t:`value` of the source :t:`owner` is copied.
00329| 
00330| #. :dp:`fls_459xx6febmf0`
00331|    The copy is assigned to the target :t:`owner`.
00332| 
00333| :dp:`fls_3xyq50abdiv6`
00334| Passing a :t:`value` :t:`by move` from a source :t:`owner` to a target
00335| :t:`owner` proceeds as follows:
00336| 
00337| #. :dp:`fls_7kcx3u8gvl1d`
00338|    The :t:`value` is unassigned from the source :t:`owner`.
00339| 
00340| #. :dp:`fls_i4hrifsb9msr`
00341|    The :t:`value` is assigned to the target :t:`owner`.
00342| 
00343| .. rubric:: Examples
00344| 
00345| :dp:`fls_7tadh1zel0fc`
00346| Type ``i32`` is a copy type. By the end of the second let statement, ``x`` is
00347| the owner of the original ``42`` and ``y`` is the owner of a cloned ``42``.
00348|

00265| A :t:`mutable variable` is a :t:`variable` whose :t:`value` can be modified.

00131| :dp:`fls_OyFwBtDGVimT`
00132| A :t:`binary crate` that is not subject to :t:`attribute` ``no_main`` shall have
00133| a :t:`function` in scope of its :t:`crate root module` under the :t:`name`
00134| ``main`` with a :t:`main function signature`.
00135| 
00136| :dp:`fls_jQqXxPyND1ds`
00137| The :t:`function` in scope of a :t:`binary crate`'s :t:`crate root module` under
00138| the :t:`name` ``main`` with a :t:`main function signature` is the :t:`binary
00139| crate`'s :t:`program entry point`.
00140| 
00141| :dp:`fls_d9nn4yuiw1ja`
00142| A :dt:`library crate` is a :t:`crate` whose :t:`crate type` is ``lib``, ``rlib``,
00143| ``staticlib``, ``dylib``, or ``cdylib``.
00144| 
00145| :dp:`fls_Mf62VqAhoZ3c`
00146| A :dt:`proc-macro crate` is a :t:`crate` whose :t:`crate type` is ``proc-macro``.
00147| 
00148| :dp:`fls_RJJmN4tP7j4m`
00149| A :t:`proc-macro crate` shall not declare :t:`[item]s` in its :t:`crate root
00150| module` with  :t:`public visibility` unless the :t:`item` is a :t:`procedural
00151| macro`.
00152| 
00153| :dp:`fls_h93C3wfbAoz1`
00154| Only a :t:`proc-macro crate` shall declare :t:`[procedural macro]s`.
00155| 
00156| .. _fls_gklst7joeo33:
00157| 
00158| Crate Imports
00159| -------------
00160| 
00161| .. rubric:: Syntax
00162| 
00163| .. syntax::
00164| 
00165|    ExternalCrateImport ::=
00166|        $$extern$$ $$crate$$ CrateIndication Renaming? $$;$$
00167| 
00168|    CrateIndication ::=
00169|        Identifier
00170|      | $$self$$
00171| 
00172| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00173| 
00174| :dp:`fls_d0pa807s5d5h`
00175| A :dt:`crate import` specifies a required dependency on an external :t:`crate`.
00176| 
00177| :dp:`fls_vfam3wzeAiah`
00178| A :dt:`crate indication` is a :t:`construct` that indicates a :t:`crate`.
00179| 
00180| :dp:`fls_2v7k7p4v0xra`
00181| A :t:`renaming` provides an alternative :t:`name` for an existing name.
00182| 
00183| :dp:`fls_ft860vkz0lkc`
00184| A :t:`crate import` binds an external :t:`crate` to its :t:`crate indication`.
00185| 
00186| :dp:`fls_k90qtnf8kgu1`
00187| :t:`Crate indication` ``self`` shall require a :t:`renaming`.
00188| 
00189| :dp:`fls_siv8bl6s2ndu`
00190| A :t:`crate import` with a :t:`renaming` with an :t:`identifier` binds the
00191| external :t:`crate` to a local :t:`name` and introduces the local :t:`name` into
00192| the enclosing :t:`scope`.
00193| 
00194| :dp:`fls_7vz5n3x6jo1s`
00195| If a :t:`crate import` appears at the :t:`crate root module`, then the
00196| :t:`crate indication` is added to the :t:`external prelude`.
00197| 
00198| :dp:`fls_3bgpc8m8yk4p`
00199| A :t:`crate indication` shall resolve to an external :t:`crate`. The process of
00200| resolving a :t:`crate indication` to an external :t:`crate` is tool-defined.
00201| 
00202| .. _fls_5w50kf83oo1u:
00203| 
00204| Compilation Roots
00205| -----------------
00206| 
00207| .. rubric:: Legality Rules

00209| :dp:`fls_fhiqvgdamq5`

Crates
------

.. rubric:: Legality Rules

:dp:`fls_qwghk79ok5h0`
A :dt:`crate` is a unit of compilation and linking that contains a tree of
nested :t:`[module]s`.

:dp:`fls_unxalgMqIr3v`
The :dt:`crate type` of a :t:`crate` is the value of the :t:`attribute`
``crate_type`` of a :t:`crate` or the value of ``--crate-type`` flag passed to
the tool compiling the :t:`crate`.

00795| 
00796| :dp:`fls_oisws5qykedi`
00797| An :t:`async block expression` denotes a new :t:`async control flow boundary`.

00802| :dp:`fls_ncd0wkgtldem`
00803| The :t:`type` of an :t:`async block expression` is a unique anonymous :t:`type`
00804| that implement the :std:`core::future::Future` trait.

00806| :dp:`fls_pvnofoomgwl5`
00807| The :t:`value` of an :t:`async block expression` is a :t:`value` of the

00814| the :t:`type` of the :t:`async block expression` that :t:`captures <capturing>`

00903| Unsafe Blocks
00904| ~~~~~~~~~~~~~
00905| 
00906| .. rubric:: Syntax
00907| 
00908| .. syntax::
00909| 
00910|    UnsafeBlockExpression ::=

00914| 
00915| :dp:`fls_p1v7k4m2t9qa`
00916| An :dt:`unsafe block` is an :t:`unsafe block expression`.

00949| Operator Expressions
00950| --------------------
00951| 
00952| .. rubric:: Syntax
00953| 
00954| .. syntax::
00955| 
00956|    OperatorExpression ::=
00957|        ArithmeticExpression
00958|      | AssignmentExpression
00959|      | BitExpression
00960|      | BorrowExpression
00961|      | ComparisonExpression
00962|      | CompoundAssignmentExpression
00963|      | DereferenceExpression
00964|      | ErrorPropagationExpression
00965|      | LazyBooleanExpression
00966|      | NegationExpression
00967|      | RawBorrowExpression
00968|      | TypeCastExpression
00969| 
00970| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00971| 
00972| :dp:`fls_ursc5ynymoy`
00973| An :dt:`operator expression` is an :t:`expression` that involves an operator.
00974| 
00975| .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
00976| 
00977| :dp:`fls_lSxXWxJn0vMO`
00978| An :t:`operator expression` that operates with :t:`[floating-point value]s` run as a :t:`constant expression` is allowed to yield different :t:`[value]s` compared to when run as a non-:t:`constant expression`.
00979| 
00980| .. _fls_qztk0bkju9u:
00981| 
00982| Borrow Expression
00983| ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
00984| 
00985| .. rubric:: Syntax
00986| 
00987| .. syntax::
00988| 
00989|    BorrowExpression ::=
00990|        $$&$$ $$mut$$? Operand
00991| 
00992| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00993| 
00994| :dp:`fls_nnqfkl228hjx`
00995| A :dt:`borrow expression` is an :t:`expression` that borrows the :t:`value` of
00996| its :t:`operand` and creates a :t:`reference` to the memory location of its
00997| :t:`operand`.
00998| 
00999| :dp:`fls_r7ix8webgqlm`
01000| An :dt:`immutable borrow expression` is a :t:`borrow expression` that lacks
01001| :t:`keyword` ``mut``.
01002| 
01003| :dp:`fls_50j167r4v61b`
01004| A :dt:`mutable borrow expression` is a :t:`borrow expression` that has
01005| :t:`keyword` ``mut``.
01006| 
01007| :dp:`fls_ya77l2zgtilp`
01008| When the :t:`operand` of a :t:`borrow expression` is a :t:`place expression`,
01009| the :t:`borrow expression` produces a :t:`reference` to the memory location
01010| indicated by the :t:`operand`. The memory location is placed in a borrowed
01011| state, or simply :dt:`borrowed`.
01012| 
01013| :dp:`fls_chr03xll75d`
01014| The :t:`type` of a :t:`borrow expression` is determined as follows:
01015| 
01016| * :dp:`fls_5b2x5ri2w54r`
01017|   If the :t:`borrow expression` denotes an :t:`immutable borrow expression`, then the
01018|   :t:`type` is ``&T``, where ``T`` is the :t:`type` of the :t:`operand`.
01019| 
01020| * :dp:`fls_agl09ia869rk`
01021|   If the :t:`borrow expression` denotes a :t:`mutable borrow expression`, then the
01022|   :t:`type` is ``&mut T``, where ``T`` is the :t:`type` of the :t:`operand`.
01023| 
01024| :dp:`fls_8cvmee9bzs40`
01025| The :t:`value` of a :t:`borrow expression` is the address of its :t:`operand`.
01026| 
01027| :dp:`fls_LuaPBicDlDTT`
01028| It is a static error if a :t:`borrow expression` would create an unaligned
01029| reference to a :t:`field` in an :t:`abstract data type` subject to
01030| :t:`attribute` :c:`repr`.
01031| 
01032| .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
01033| 
01034| :dp:`fls_2jd0mgw4zja4`
01035| The :t:`evaluation` of a :t:`borrow expression` evaluates its :t:`operand`.
01036| 
01037| .. rubric:: Examples
01038| 
01039| .. code-block:: rust
01040| 
01041|    let mut answer = 42;
01042| 
01043| :dp:`fls_350qejoq9i23`
01044| Mutable borrow.
01045| 
01046| .. syntax::
01047| 
01048|    let ref_answer = &mut answer;
01049| 
01050| .. _fls_vXGuvRWOLbEE:
01051| 
01052| Raw Borrow Expression
01053| ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
01054| 
01055| .. rubric:: Syntax
01056| 
01057| .. syntax::
01058| 
01059|    RawBorrowExpression ::=
01060|        $$&$$ $$raw$$ ($$const$$ | $$mut$$) Operand
01061| 
01062| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
01063| 
01064| :dp:`fls_TS6DvMon5h27`
01065| A :dt:`raw borrow expression` is an :t:`expression` that creates a :t:`raw pointer` to the memory location of its :t:`operand` without incurring a :t:`borrow`.
01066| 
01067| :dp:`fls_UtjWrE2qeplQ`
01068| An :dt:`immutable raw borrow expression` is a :t:`raw borrow expression` that has :t:`keyword` ``const``.
01069| 
01070| :dp:`fls_4e7EE4a8Yvmy`
01071| A :dt:`mutable raw borrow expression` is a :t:`raw borrow expression` that has :t:`keyword` ``mut``.
01072| 
01073| :dp:`fls_gOXUWePymgGV`
01074| When the :t:`operand` of a :t:`raw borrow expression` is a :t:`place expression`, the :t:`raw borrow expression` produces a :t:`raw pointer` to the memory location indicated by the :t:`operand`.
01075| 
01076| :dp:`fls_YBC8GrIBzZbi`
01077| It is a static error if the :t:`operand` of a :t:`raw borrow expression` is a :t:`temporary`.
01078| 
01079| :dp:`fls_Twkre8IzUa8S`
01080| The :t:`type` of a :t:`raw borrow expression` is determined as follows:
01081| 
01082| * :dp:`fls_Ki4FOzJMqtvJ`
01083|   If the :t:`raw borrow expression` denotes an :t:`immutable raw borrow expression`, then the :t:`type` is ``*const T``, where ``T`` is the :t:`type` of the :t:`operand`.
01084| 
01085| * :dp:`fls_DJxQDBsO9hc7`
01086|   If the :t:`raw borrow expression` denotes a :t:`mutable raw borrow expression`, then the :t:`type` is ``*mut T``, where ``T`` is the :t:`type` of the :t:`operand`.
01087| 
01088| :dp:`fls_WlXB0AHifCdd`
01089| The :t:`value` of a :t:`raw borrow expression` is the address of its :t:`operand`.
01090| 
01091| .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
01092| 
01093| :dp:`fls_qQrV8QuGGcVO`
01094| The :t:`evaluation` of a :t:`raw borrow expression` evaluates its :t:`operand`.
01095| 
01096| .. rubric:: Examples
01097| 
01098| .. code-block:: rust
01099| 
01100|    let mut answer = 42;
01101| 
01102| :dp:`fls_dTABiwAPGhdZ`
01103| Mutable raw borrow.
01104| 
01105| .. syntax::
01106| 
01107|    let ref_answer = &raw mut answer;
01108| 
01109| .. _fls_5cm4gkt55hjh:
01110| 
01111| Dereference Expression
01112| ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
01113| 
01114| .. rubric:: Syntax
01115| 
01116| .. syntax::
01117| 
01118|    DereferenceExpression ::=
01119|        $$*$$ Operand
01120| 
01121| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
01122| 
01123| :dp:`fls_f6wktzofzdn1`
01124| A :dt:`dereference expression` is an :t:`expression` that obtains the pointed-to
01125| memory location of its :t:`operand`.
01126| 
01127| :dp:`fls_aOXtV5sEUGxJ`
01128| A :dt:`dereference` is the memory location produced by evaluating a
01129| :t:`dereference expression`.
01130| 
01131| :dp:`fls_aeh5pzpcjveq`
01132| When the :t:`operand` of a :t:`dereference expression` is of a :t:`pointer
01133| type`, the :t:`dereference expression` denotes the pointed-to memory location of
01134| the :t:`operand`, or the :t:`dereference` of the :t:`operand`.
01135| 
01136| :dp:`fls_9cc0ml2sru6x`
01137| The :t:`dereference` is assignable when the :t:`dereference expression` is a
01138| :t:`mutable place expression`.
01139| 
01140| :dp:`fls_8i4jzksxlrw0`
01141| Dereferencing a :t:`raw pointer` shall require :t:`unsafe context` unless the :t:`dereference expression` is the :t:`operand` of a :t:`raw borrow expression`.
01142| 
01143| :dp:`fls_d68ddlse4zp`
01144| If the context of a :t:`dereference expression` is an
01145| :t:`immutable place expression context`, then the :t:`dereference expression`
01146| is equivalent to :t:`expression` ``*core::ops::Deref::deref(&operand)``.
01147| 
01148| :dp:`fls_g73vguanbs1x`
01149| If the context of a :t:`dereference expression` is a
01150| :t:`mutable place expression context`, then the :t:`dereference expression`
01151| is equivalent to :t:`expression`
01152| ``*core::ops::DerefMut::deref_mut(&mut operand)``.
01153| 
01154| :dp:`fls_8ibfqxtnahzx`
01155| The :t:`type` of a :t:`dereference expression` is determined as follows:
01156| 
01157| * :dp:`fls_7e7tka4f2f1a`
01158|   If the :t:`type` of the :t:`operand` is ``&mut T``, ``&T``, ``*mut T``, or
01159|   ``*const T``, then the :t:`type` is ``T``.
01160| 
01161| * :dp:`fls_y9bc691kkh6v`
01162|   Otherwise the :t:`type` is :t:`associated type`
01163|   :std:`core::ops::Deref::Target`.
01164| 
01165| :dp:`fls_gw49nukfveib`
01166| The :t:`value` of a :t:`dereference expression` is determined as follows:
01167| 
01168| * :dp:`fls_jjf3sz9ddfhy`
01169|   If the :t:`type` of the :t:`operand` is ``&mut T``, ``&T``, ``*mut T``, or
01170|   ``*const T``, then the :t:`value` is the pointed-to :t:`value`.
01171| 
01172| * :dp:`fls_fyend8kkpqq4`
01173|   Otherwise the :t:`value` is the result of evaluating :t:`expression`
01174|   ``*core::ops::Deref::deref(&operand)`` or :t:`expression`
01175|   ``*core::ops::DerefMut::deref_mut(&mut operand)`` respectively.
01176| 
01177| .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
01178| 
01179| :dp:`fls_72bpdsxxbgeq`
01180| The :t:`evaluation` of a :t:`dereference expression` evaluates its :t:`operand`.
01181| 
01182| .. rubric:: Undefined Behavior
01183| 
01184| :dp:`fls_9wgldua1u8yt`
01185| It is undefined behavior to dereference a :t:`raw pointer` that is either
01186| :dt:`dangling` or unaligned.
01187| 
01188| .. rubric:: Examples
01189|

01191| See :p:`fls_350qejoq9i23` for the declaration of ``ref_answer``.
01192| 
01193| .. code-block:: rust

01195|    let deref_answer = *ref_answer;

05012| 
05013| Await Expressions
05014| -----------------
05015| 
05016| .. rubric:: Syntax
05017| 
05018| .. syntax::
05019| 
05020|    AwaitExpression ::=
05021|        FutureOperand $$.$$ $$await$$
05022| 
05023|    FutureOperand ::=
05024|        Operand
05025| 
05026| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
05027| 
05028| :dp:`fls_sjz5s71hwm7l`
05029| An :dt:`await expression` is an :t:`expression` that polls a :t:`future`,
05030| suspending the :t:`execution` of the :t:`future` until the :t:`future` is ready.
05031| 
05032| :dp:`fls_vhchgab59jvd`
05033| A :dt:`future operand` is an :t:`operand` whose :t:`future` is being awaited by
05034| an :t:`await expression`.
05035| 
05036| :dp:`fls_k9pncajmhgk1`
05037| An :t:`await expression` shall appear within an
05038| :t:`async control flow boundary`. Only the innermost :t:`control flow boundary`
05039| shall be considered.
05040| 
05041| :dp:`fls_9uw5pd7kbrx3`
05042| The :t:`type` of a :t:`future operand` shall implement the
05043| :std:`core::future::IntoFuture` :t:`trait`.
05044| 
05045| :dp:`fls_c6mxfzef2qop`
05046| The :t:`type` of an :t:`await expression` is
05047| ``<_ as core::future::IntoFuture>::Output``.
05048| 
05049| :dp:`fls_l396mo6k9ox7`
05050| The :t:`value` of an :t:`await expression` is the :t:`value` held by
05051| :std:`core::task::Poll::Ready`.
05052| 
05053| .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
05054| 
05055| :dp:`fls_1ppywe40s62c`
05056| The :t:`evaluation` of an :t:`await expression` proceeds as follows:
05057| 
05058| #. :dp:`fls_eymcs2rgv3qw`
05059|    The :t:`future operand` is evaluated to a :t:`temporary` by invoking
05060|    :std:`core::future::IntoFuture::into_future` with the :t:`future operand`.
05061| 
05062| #. :dp:`fls_qshnnpirkasz`
05063|    The :t:`temporary` is pinned using :std:`core::pin::Pin::new_unchecked`.
05064| 
05065| #. :dp:`fls_umevprl99y6c`
05066|    The pinned :t:`temporary` is polled using :std:`core::future::Future::poll`,
05067|    passing in the :std:`core::task::Context` of the current task.
05068| 
05069| #. :dp:`fls_k76d8ady623m`
05070|    If :std:`core::future::Future::poll` returns
05071|    :std:`core::task::Poll::Pending`, then the current :t:`future` yields.
05072| 
05073| #. :dp:`fls_frwtufwe8dya`
05074|    If :std:`core::future::Future::poll` returns :std:`core::task::Poll::Ready`,
05075|    then
05076| 
05077|    #. :dp:`fls_u72ylhlmqge3`
05078|       The :t:`value` held within is unwrapped.
05079| 
05080|    #. :dp:`fls_tn3vwidct3ks`
05081|       Control stops the evaluation of the :t:`await expression`.
05082| 
05083| .. rubric:: Examples
05084| 
05085| .. code-block:: rust
05086| 
05087|    let future = async { expensive_function() };
05088|    future.await;
05089| 
05090| .. _fls_kw25194gpael:
05091| 
05092| Expression Precedence
05093| ---------------------
05094| 
05095| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
05096| 
05097| :dp:`fls_cwt7afsbgs7w`
05098| Certain :t:`[expression]s` are subject to :t:`precedence` and
05099| :t:`associativity`.
05100| 
05101| :dp:`fls_ya23jjg5wjl`
05102| :dt:`Precedence` is the order by which :t:`[expression]s` are evaluated in the
05103| presence of other :t:`[expression]s`.
05104| 
05105| :dp:`fls_bezkcuwp5qol`
05106| :dt:`Associativity` is the order by which :t:`[operand]s` are evaluated within a
05107| single :t:`expression`.
05108| 
05109| :dp:`fls_48br7odx6nke`
05110| The :t:`precedence` and :t:`associativity` of qualifying :t:`[expression]s` are
05111| as follows:
05112| 
05113| .. list-table::
05114| 
05115|    * - :dp:`fls_mk2yk99p6nvp`
05116|      - **Expression**
05117|      - **Precedence**
05118|      - **Associativity**
05119|    * - :dp:`fls_jtdnf0vmn6xt`
05120|      - :t:`Array expression`
05121| 
05122|        :t:`Block expression`
05123| 
05124|        :t:`Continue expression`

00896|    ConfigurationPredicate ::=
00897|        ConfigurationOption
00898|      | ConfigurationPredicateAll
00899|      | ConfigurationPredicateAny
00900|      | ConfigurationPredicateNot
00901|      | $$true$$
00902|      | $$false$$
00903| 
00904|    ConfigurationOption ::=
00905|        ConfigurationOptionName ConfigurationOptionValue?
00906| 
00907|    ConfigurationOptionName ::=
00908|        Identifier
00909| 
00910|    ConfigurationOptionValue ::=
00911|        $$=$$ StringLiteral
00912| 
00913|    ConfigurationPredicateAll ::=
00914|        $$all$$ $$($$ ConfigurationPredicateList? $$)$$
00915| 
00916|    ConfigurationPredicateAny ::=
00917|        $$any$$ $$($$ ConfigurationPredicateList? $$)$$
00918| 
00919|    ConfigurationPredicateNot ::=
00920|        $$not$$ $$($$ ConfigurationPredicate $$)$$
00921| 
00922|    ConfigurationPredicateList ::=
00923|        ConfigurationPredicate ($$,$$ ConfigurationPredicate)* $$,$$?
00924| 
00925| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00926| 
00927| :dp:`fls_xrjp7xw9jutz`
00928| :t:`Attribute` :dc:`cfg` enables :t:`conditional compilation`.
00929| 
00930| :dp:`fls_l96kyix5xsof`
00931| A :dt:`configuration predicate` is a :t:`construct` that evaluates statically
00932| to either ``true`` or ``false``, and controls :t:`conditional compilation`.
00933| 
00934| :dp:`fls_y1MUhnKCxK6T`
00935| An :dt:`all configuration predicate` is a :t:`configuration predicate` that
00936| models existential quantifier ALL.
00937| 
00938| :dp:`fls_tncxxsyutppf`
00939| An :t:`all configuration predicate` evaluates statically to ``true`` when either
00940| all nested configuration predicates evaluate to ``true``, or there are no nested
00941| configuration predicates.
00942| 
00943| :dp:`fls_Rp73YEE3aFdI`
00944| An :dt:`any configuration predicate` is a :t:`configuration predicate` that
00945| models existential quantifier ANY.
00946| 
00947| :dp:`fls_m0zxktz168e0`
00948| An :t:`any configuration predicate` evaluates statically to ``true`` when any
00949| nested configuration predicate evaluates to ``true``.
00950| 
00951| :dp:`fls_XsxeOd32JI8x`
00952| A :dt:`not configuration predicate` is a :t:`configuration predicate` that
00953| negates the Boolean :t:`value` of its nested :t:`configuration predicate`.
00954| 
00955| :dp:`fls_tvsadfy9uibu`
00956| A :t:`not configuration predicate` evaluates statically to ``true`` when its
00957| nested configuration predicate evaluates to ``false``.
00958| 
00959| :dp:`fls_JWx8vQwl19Fu`
00960| :t:`Configuration predicate` :dc:`true` always evaluates statically to ``true``.
00961| 
00962| :dp:`fls_SziFAQsio0ab`
00963| :t:`Configuration predicate` :dc:`false` always evaluates statically to ``false``.
00964| 
00965| :dp:`fls_jbl9xyynjo0g`
00966| The :t:`evaluation` of a configuration option is tool-defined.
00967|

.. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT OR Apache-2.0

.. default-domain:: spec

.. _fls_l21tjqjkkaa0:

Associated Items
================

.. rubric:: Syntax

.. syntax::

   AssociatedItem ::=
       OuterAttributeOrDoc* (AssociatedItemWithVisibility | TerminatedMacroInvocation)

   AssociatedItemWithVisibility ::=
       VisibilityModifier? (
           ConstantDeclaration
         | FunctionDeclaration
         | TypeAliasDeclaration
       )

.. rubric:: Legality Rules

:dp:`fls_ckzd25qd213t`
An :dt:`associated item` is an :t:`item` that appears within an
:t:`implementation` or a :t:`trait`.

:dp:`fls_8cz4rdrklaj4`
An :dt:`associated type` is a :t:`type alias` that appears as an
:t:`associated item`.

:dp:`fls_nNisRZ6p4T6u`
An :dt:`incomplete associated constant` is an :t:`associated constant` without
a :t:`constant initializer`.

:dp:`fls_nMEacKMoaVn0`
An :dt:`incomplete associated function` is an :t:`associated function` without
a :t:`function body`.

:dp:`fls_FJEqMV7Qt6ZJ`
An :dt:`incomplete associated type` is an :t:`associated type` without an
:t:`initialization type`.

:dp:`fls_w8nu8suy7t5`
An :t:`associated type` shall not be used in the :t:`path expression` of a
:t:`struct expression`.

:dp:`fls_wasocqdnuzd1`
An :t:`associated type` with a :s:`TypeBoundList` shall appear only as an
:t:`associated trait type`.

:dp:`fls_PeD0DzjK57be`
A :dt:`generic associated type` is an :t:`associated type` with
:t:`[generic parameter]s`.

:dp:`fls_3foYUch29ZtF`
A :t:`lifetime parameter` of a :t:`generic associated type` requires a
:t:`bound` of the form ``T: 'lifetime``, where ``T`` is a :t:`type parameter`
or :c:`Self` and ``'lifetime`` is the :t:`lifetime parameter`, when

* :dp:`fls_SnQc0zZS57Cz`
  The :t:`generic associated type` is used in an :t:`associated function` of
  the same :t:`trait`, and

* :dp:`fls_6Z05BK2JSzpP`
  The corresponding :t:`lifetime argument` in the use is not the ``'static``
  :t:`lifetime` and has either an explicit :t:`bound` or an :t:`implied bound`
  that constrains the :t:`type parameter`, and

* :dp:`fls_AtItgS1UvwiX`
  The intersection of all such uses is not empty.

:dp:`fls_4ftfefcotb4g`
An :t:`associated implementation constant` shall have a :t:`constant
initializer`.

:dp:`fls_qb5qpfe0uwk`
An :dt:`associated implementation function` is an :t:`associated function` that
appears within an :t:`implementation`.

:dp:`fls_1zlkeb6fz10j`
An :t:`associated implementation function` shall have a :t:`function body`.

:dp:`fls_tw8u0cc5867l`
An :dt:`associated implementation type` is an :t:`associated type` that appears
within an :t:`implementation`.

:dp:`fls_bx7931x4155h`
An :t:`associated implementation type` shall have an :t:`initialization type`.

:dp:`fls_bnTcCbDvdp94`
An :dt:`associated trait item` is an :t:`associated item` that appears
within a :t:`trait`.

:dp:`fls_N3cdn4lCZ2Bf`
An :dt:`associated trait implementation item` is an :t:`associated item` that
appears within a :t:`trait implementation`.

:dp:`fls_x564isbhobym`
An :t:`associated trait constant` is an :t:`associated constant` that appears
within a :t:`trait`.

:dp:`fls_b6nns7oqvdpm`
An :dt:`associated trait function` is an :t:`associated function` that appears
within a :t:`trait`.

:dp:`fls_2TRwCz38kuRz`
An :t:`associated trait function` shall not be subject to :t:`keyword` ``const``.

:dp:`fls_WnsVATJvUdza`
Every occurrence of an :t:`impl trait type` in the :t:`return type` of an
:t:`associated trait function` is equivalent to referring to a new
anonymous :t:`associated trait type` of the :t:`implemented trait`.

:dp:`fls_yyhebj4qyk34`
An :dt:`associated trait type` is an :t:`associated type` that appears within
a :t:`trait`.

:dp:`fls_kl9p3ycl5mzf`
An :t:`associated trait type` shall not have an :t:`initialization type`.

:dp:`fls_a5prbmuruma4`
An :t:`associated trait type` has an implicit :std:`core::marker::Sized`
:t:`bound`.

:dp:`fls_vp2ov6ykueue`
An :t:`associated trait type` of the form

.. code-block:: rust

   	trait T {
   	    type X: Bound;
   	}

:dp:`fls_5uf74nvdm64o`
is equivalent to a :t:`where clause` of the following form:

.. code-block:: rust

   	trait T where Self::X: Bound {
   	    type X;
   	}

:dp:`fls_amWtS80fPtza`
An :dt:`associated trait implementation function` is an :t:`associated function`
that appears within a :t:`trait implementation`.

:dp:`fls_Cu8FWrisrqz1`
Every occurrence of an :t:`impl trait type` in the :t:`return type` of an
:t:`associated trait implementation function` is equivalent to referring to the
corresponding :t:`associated trait type` of the corresponding :t:`associated
trait function`.

:dp:`fls_oy92gzxgc273`
A :t:`method` is an :t:`associated function` with a :t:`self parameter`.

:dp:`fls_WXnCWfJGoQx3`
The type of a :t:`self parameter` shall be one of the following:

* :dp:`fls_OaszUw4IFobz`
  A :t:`type specification` resolving to the :t:`implementing type` of the
  related :t:`implementation`, or

* :dp:`fls_Wd2FZRomB5yn`
  ``&T`` where ``T`` is one of the :t:`[type]s` listed in this enumeration,
  or

* :dp:`fls_lcEyToYIlcmf`
  ``&mut T`` where ``T`` is one of the :t:`[type]s` listed in this
  enumeration, or

* :dp:`fls_IKSPR7ZQMErU`
  :std:`core::pin::Pin<T> <core::pin::Pin>` where ``T`` is one of the :t:`[type]s` listed in this
  enumeration.

:dp:`fls_oHxzyaiT7Qm6`
The :t:`visibility modifier` of an :t:`associated trait item` or :t:`associated
trait implementation item` is rejected, but may still be consumed by
:t:`[macro]s`.

.. rubric:: Examples

.. code-block:: rust

   trait Greeter {
       const MAX_GREETINGS: i32;

       fn greet(self, message: &str);
   }

   struct Implementor {
       delivered_greetings: i32
   }

   impl Greeter for Implementor {
       const MAX_GREETINGS: i32 = 42;

       fn greet(mut self, message: &str) {
           if self.delivered_greetings < Self::MAX_GREETINGS {
               self.delivered_greetings += 1;
               println!("{}", message);
           }
       }
   }

:dp:`fls_znfADVeOvXHD`
Generic associated type with lifetime bound.

.. code-block:: rust

   trait LendingIterator {
       type Item<'x> where Self: 'x;

       fn next<'a>(&'a mut self) -> Self::Item<'a>;
   }

is equivalent to a :t:`where clause` of the following form:

.. code-block:: rust

   	trait T where Self::X: Bound {
   	    type X;
   	}

:dp:`fls_amWtS80fPtza`
An :dt:`associated trait implementation function` is an :t:`associated function`
that appears within a :t:`trait implementation`.

:dp:`fls_Cu8FWrisrqz1`
Every occurrence of an :t:`impl trait type` in the :t:`return type` of an
:t:`associated trait implementation function` is equivalent to referring to the
corresponding :t:`associated trait type` of the corresponding :t:`associated
trait function`.

:dp:`fls_oy92gzxgc273`
A :t:`method` is an :t:`associated function` with a :t:`self parameter`.

:dp:`fls_WXnCWfJGoQx3`
The type of a :t:`self parameter` shall be one of the following:

* :dp:`fls_OaszUw4IFobz`
  A :t:`type specification` resolving to the :t:`implementing type` of the
  related :t:`implementation`, or

* :dp:`fls_Wd2FZRomB5yn`
  ``&T`` where ``T`` is one of the :t:`[type]s` listed in this enumeration,
  or

* :dp:`fls_lcEyToYIlcmf`
  ``&mut T`` where ``T`` is one of the :t:`[type]s` listed in this
  enumeration, or

* :dp:`fls_IKSPR7ZQMErU`
  :std:`core::pin::Pin<T> <core::pin::Pin>` where ``T`` is one of the :t:`[type]s` listed in this
  enumeration.

:dp:`fls_oHxzyaiT7Qm6`
The :t:`visibility modifier` of an :t:`associated trait item` or :t:`associated
trait implementation item` is rejected, but may still be consumed by
:t:`[macro]s`.

.. rubric:: Examples

.. code-block:: rust

   trait Greeter {
       const MAX_GREETINGS: i32;

       fn greet(self, message: &str);
   }

   struct Implementor {
       delivered_greetings: i32
   }

   impl Greeter for Implementor {
       const MAX_GREETINGS: i32 = 42;

       fn greet(mut self, message: &str) {
           if self.delivered_greetings < Self::MAX_GREETINGS {
               self.delivered_greetings += 1;
               println!("{}", message);
           }
       }
   }

:dp:`fls_znfADVeOvXHD`
Generic associated type with lifetime bound.

.. code-block:: rust

   trait LendingIterator {
       type Item<'x> where Self: 'x;

       fn next<'a>(&'a mut self) -> Self::Item<'a>;
   }
